'''Karle Hammond''' (born 25 April 1974) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] (two spells), and in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], and the [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax Blue Sox]], as a {{rlp|so|lf}} or {{rlp|ce}}.

==Background==
Hammond was born in [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire]], England.

==Career==
===Club===
Hammond started his career at Widnes before signing for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in September 1995 for a fee of £85,000.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Ban doubled for drug use |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/ban-doubled-for-drug-use-1600734.html |website=The Independent |access-date=16 February 2024 |date=11 September 1995}}</ref> He played {{rlp|so}} in St Helens' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[John Smith's Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref name="13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|accessdate=1 January 2015|archive-date=7 October 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Hammond played as a {{rlp|so}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] 40–32 victory over the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 27 April 1996 in front of a crowd of 78,550.<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|accessdate=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> He also played in the [[1997 Challenge Cup]] final (also against Bradford Bulls) and scored a try in their 32–22 win.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Martyn grabs Bulls by the horns |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-martyn-grabs-bulls-by-the-horns-1259755.html |website=The Independent |access-date=17 February 2024 |date=3 May 1997}}</ref>

Hammond left Saints when his contract expired at the end of the 1998 season, and signed a two-year deal with [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: McRae leaves job with Britain |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-mcrae-leaves-job-with-britain-1201389.html |website=The Independent |access-date=16 February 2024 |date=29 September 1998}}</ref> He played in the team's [[1999 Challenge Cup]] final defeat against [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sport: Rugby League  Challenge Cup teams and profiles |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/sport/rugby_league/332277.stm |website=BBC News |access-date=17 February 2024 |date=1 May 1999}}</ref>

He then briefly returned to Widnes before joining [[Salford City Reds]] in March 2001.<ref name="Reds snap up Hammond">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1224628.stm|title=Reds snap up Hammond|publisher=BBC|date=2001-03-16|access-date=2008-02-28}}</ref> He spent the following season with [[Halifax Blue Sox]], but was released in August 2002.<ref>{{cite web |title=Halifax boot out Hammond |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2211312.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=17 February 2024 |date=23 August 2002}}</ref>

===International===
He was the top try-scorer of the [[1996 Great Britain Lions tour]] of Papua New Guinea, Fiji and New Zealand.

He was selected to play for Wales in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]], but was forced to withdraw from the squad due to injury.<ref>{{cite web |title=Hammond hammer blow for Wales |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/world_cup_2000/group_2/976014.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=16 February 2024 |date=17 October 2000}}</ref>
